prd_meta.title: Semantic Caching: single-layer analysis response cache via pgvector
prd_meta.description: One semantic cache at the analysis level. On hit, skip the entire pipeline (classify + search decision + data fetch + LLM generation). On miss, run everything normally. No intermediate step caching. pgvector for similarity search, Redis for conversation/task state (unchanged).
source_documents: Notion backlog: Use Redis AI for better caching (342c1f5aa05480ca89c7d0ba37ec3a7c)
prd_meta.baseline_policy: Before each phase, record fresh timing using the shared measurement context. After implementation, compare cold (cache miss) vs warm (cache hit). seconds_saved = cold_duration - warm_duration.

default_measurement_context.question: What is Apple's revenue trend over the last 3 years?
default_measurement_context.paraphrase: How has AAPL revenue changed in the past 3 years?
default_measurement_context.notes: Run original question (cold miss → full pipeline). Then run paraphrase (should hit semantic cache). Compare total wall-clock.

design_decisions.architecture: Single semantic cache layer at the analysis response level. No classification or search decision caching.
design_decisions.rationale: Users rarely ask identical questions, so exact-match caching has negligible hit rate. Semantic cache catches paraphrases. Caching intermediate steps (classify, search decision) adds complexity for marginal gain — the analysis cache already skips them on hit.
design_decisions.embedding_timing: Background async — embed + store after response completes, no added latency to user
design_decisions.similarity_threshold: cosine distance < 0.20 (similarity > 0.80) — originally 0.08, raised after prod testing showed real paraphrases land at 0.10-0.18 with text-embedding-3-small
design_decisions.ttl_strategy: Tiered — historical period questions = 7d, recent/latest quarter = 24h (+ crawl invalidation), price/market = 1h
design_decisions.model_preference_in_cache: Ignored — cache is model-agnostic for higher hit rate
design_decisions.cache_scope: Per-ticker. Question + ticker together determine cache identity.

phases.title: pgvector table + embedding infrastructure
phases.definition: Enable pgvector extension, create semantic_cache table, build the SemanticCache class with embed/store/lookup/invalidate methods, and add the question normalizer + TTL tier detector.
phases.explanation_for_agents: Schema: semantic_cache(id serial, ticker varchar, question_text text, question_embedding vector(1536), answer_text text, sources jsonb, model_used varchar, ttl_tier varchar, created_at timestamptz, expires_at timestamptz). Index: HNSW on question_embedding (m=16, ef_construction=64). Btree composite on (ticker, expires_at). SemanticCache class: embed(text) → OpenAI text-embedding-3-small 1536-dim vector (no normalization — raw text preserves semantic similarity better); store(...) → inserts with TTL tier-based expires_at; lookup(ticker, embedding, threshold=0.20) → ORM query with cosine_distance, filter by ticker + expires_at > now(); invalidate_ticker(ticker) → DELETE WHERE ticker. TTL detection via regex: year/quarter → HISTORICAL (7d), price/market → MARKET (1h), default → RECENT (24h).
files_modified: models/semantic_cache.py — SQLAlchemy model with pgvector Vector column

implementation_checks: pgvector extension enabled on Railway Postgres
semantic_cache table created via migration
SemanticCache.embed() returns 1536-dim vector from OpenAI
SemanticCache.store() inserts row with correct expires_at and ttl_tier
SemanticCache.lookup() returns cached row when cosine distance < 0.20, None otherwise
SemanticCache.invalidate_ticker() deletes all rows for ticker
TTL tier detection covers: HISTORICAL, RECENT, MARKET with regex tests

verification.smoke_test: All verified against prod DB: store → paraphrase hit → unrelated miss → wrong ticker miss → invalidate → post-invalidation miss. 13 unit tests pass. Lint clean.
verification.what_to_measure: embed() latency (expect ~100ms), lookup() latency (expect <20ms), store() latency (expect <5ms)

learnings.threshold_calibration: PRD threshold of 0.08 was too tight for text-embedding-3-small. Real paraphrase distances: identical=0.00, paraphrases=0.10-0.18, different tickers=0.35. Raised to 0.20.
learnings.no_normalize_before_embed: Lowercasing before embedding degrades similarity (0.25 vs 0.10 for paraphrases). 'Apple' vs 'AAPL' diverge more when both lowercased. Embed raw text, normalize only for display/storage.
learnings.pgvector_cast_required: Raw SQL with str(embedding) silently returns wrong distances. Must use cast(:embedding as vector) or use pgvector's ORM cosine_distance() method.
learnings.hnsw_over_ivfflat: IVFFlat with lists=100 on an empty table is useless — needs training data. HNSW works from zero rows with better recall.
learnings.index_cleanup: Dropped standalone ticker and expires_at indexes — composite (ticker, expires_at) covers both lookup and invalidation queries.

phases.title: Integrate semantic cache into /analyze pipeline
phases.estimated_effect: 5-15s saved per request on cache hit (skips entire pipeline)
phases.definition: Wire SemanticCache into the analyze endpoint. On request: embed question → lookup → if hit, re-stream cached response as SSE. On miss: run full pipeline, then background-store the response for future hits.
phases.explanation_for_agents: Integration points in main.py analyze_financial_data(): (1) BEFORE routing to FinancialAnalyzer/ETFAnalyzer, call semantic_cache.lookup(ticker, embedding). The embedding call is sync here (~100ms) because we need it for lookup. (2) On HIT: emit SSE events from cached data — conversation event, then chunk answer_text into ~100-char pieces emitted as answer events (with ~5ms delays for natural streaming feel), then sources event, then model_used event. Skip all classification, search decision, data fetch, LLM generation. (3) On MISS: run full pipeline normally. After the response stream completes (all chunks collected), fire background task to embed + store. The answer text needs to be collected during streaming — add a buffer that accumulates answer chunks alongside normal SSE emission. (4) For conversation context: cached responses should still be added to the conversation store (Redis) so follow-up questions have context. (5) Add a 'cached' field to the model_used SSE event so frontend can optionally display it. (6) Handle ETF and company paths identically — cache is ticker-scoped, agnostic to handler type.
files_to_modify: main.py — cache lookup before pipeline, answer buffer during streaming, background store after response
connectors/semantic_cache.py — may need a convenience method that combines embed+lookup in one call

implementation_checks: Cache hit re-streams SSE events identical in structure to live response
Cache miss runs full pipeline unchanged, stores response in background
Conversation store updated even on cache hit (follow-up questions work)
model_used event includes cached: true/false field
ETF and company analysis both cache-enabled
Background store is fire-and-forget (failure doesn't affect user response)

verification.smoke_test: Run /analyze with 'What is Apple's revenue trend over the last 3 years?' (cold). Record total wall-clock. Wait 2s for background embed+store. Run paraphrase 'How has AAPL revenue changed in the past 3 years?' — should hit semantic cache. Verify: (1) wall-clock < 1s, (2) SSE events have same structure, (3) model_used shows cached: true, (4) follow-up question 'Compare to Microsoft' still works (conversation context preserved).
verification.what_to_measure: Total request wall-clock: cold (full pipeline) vs semantic hit

phases.title: Invalidate semantic cache on crawl completion
phases.estimated_effect: Correctness improvement — prevents stale analysis after new financial data arrives
phases.definition: When a crawl job persists new financial data for a ticker, delete all semantic_cache rows for that ticker so subsequent questions get fresh analysis.
phases.explanation_for_agents: Find where crawl tasks commit new financial data to Postgres (likely in tasks/ or connectors/). After the successful DB commit, call semantic_cache.invalidate_ticker(ticker). This deletes all cached rows for that ticker regardless of TTL tier — the tiered TTL handles time-based expiry, but crawl invalidation handles data-change-based expiry. This is a simple DELETE WHERE ticker = $1 query. Make it fire-and-forget with try/except logging — crawl should never fail because cache invalidation failed. HISTORICAL tier questions (e.g. '2023 revenue') technically don't need invalidation since historical data doesn't change, but it's simpler to invalidate everything than to filter by tier.
files_to_modify: Crawl task completion handlers — add invalidate_ticker() call after data persist
connectors/semantic_cache.py — invalidate_ticker() already built in phase 1

implementation_checks: After crawl completes for AAPL, all semantic_cache rows with ticker='AAPL' are deleted
Invalidation is fire-and-forget (crawl doesn't fail if Postgres delete fails)
Logged when invalidation runs and how many rows deleted

verification.smoke_test: Populate semantic cache by running /analyze for AAPL. Verify rows exist: SELECT count(*) FROM semantic_cache WHERE ticker='AAPL'. Trigger or simulate crawl completion for AAPL. Verify count is 0. Run /analyze again — full pipeline runs (cache miss).
verification.what_to_measure: Row count before/after invalidation, verify next request is a cache miss

phases.title: Pre-computed analysis snippets for popular tickers
phases.estimated_effect: Improved quality + 2-5s faster generation (LLM gets pre-analyzed data points in context, less reasoning needed)
phases.definition: Background job generates common financial analysis snippets for most-viewed tickers after each crawl. Snippets are injected into the analysis prompt as pre-computed context. The LLM still generates a custom response but with pre-verified calculations available.
phases.explanation_for_agents: This is NOT a cache — it's context enrichment. Snippets are compact summaries (< 200 tokens each) of common financial data points, pre-calculated correctly. They reduce hallucination risk for numerical data and speed up generation because the LLM can reference pre-computed figures instead of deriving them from raw financial statements. Flow: crawl completes for ticker → Celery task generates snippets → stored in Redis as precomputed:{ticker}:{snippet_type} → context builders inject them into prompt. Snippet types: (1) revenue_trend — last 4Q revenue + YoY growth rates; (2) profitability — gross/operating/net margins for last 4Q; (3) balance_sheet — debt-to-equity, current ratio, cash position. Generation: run standardized queries through the financial data connectors (NOT through LLM — pure computation from DB data). Most-viewed tickers available via existing endpoint. TTL = until next crawl (Redis key overwritten on regeneration).
files_to_modify: New: services/precompute.py — snippet generation from DB data (pure computation, no LLM)
New: tasks/precompute_snippets.py — Celery task triggered after crawl
services/question_analyzer/context_builders/ — inject snippets into prompt when available
connectors/cache.py — get/set for precomputed:{ticker}:{type} Redis keys

implementation_checks: Snippets generated from DB data, no LLM calls (pure computation)
3 snippet types: revenue_trend, profitability, balance_sheet
Each snippet < 200 tokens
Injected into context builder as 'Pre-computed data points' section
Missing snippets are graceful — analysis works fine without them
Generated for top 10 most-viewed tickers after each crawl

verification.smoke_test: Trigger snippet generation for AAPL. Verify Redis keys exist. Run /analyze with AAPL revenue question. Check Langfuse to confirm snippets in prompt. Run same question without snippets (delete Redis keys). Compare: (1) generation duration with vs without, (2) numerical accuracy on 5 test questions (spot-check revenue figures, margin calculations).
verification.what_to_measure: Main LLM generation duration with vs without snippets. Qualitative: numerical accuracy.

phases.title: LLM-based TTL tier classification
phases.estimated_effect: Better cache expiry accuracy for ambiguous questions
phases.definition: Replace regex-based detect_ttl_tier() with an LLM classifier that understands temporal intent. Regex misses nuanced cases like 'before the pandemic' (HISTORICAL), 'right now' (MARKET), or 'compare last quarter to the one before' (RECENT).
phases.explanation_for_agents: Current detect_ttl_tier() in connectors/semantic_cache.py uses regex patterns to match year/quarter (HISTORICAL), price/market keywords (MARKET), or defaults to RECENT. This works for ~80% of questions but fails on indirect temporal references. Replace with a lightweight LLM call (gemini-2.5-flash or similar fast model) that classifies the question into one of three tiers. Key tradeoff: adds ~200ms latency + cost per cache store operation. Since TTL tier only affects cache expiry duration (not correctness), misclassification is low-impact — a wrong tier just means slightly earlier/later expiry. Implementation: (1) keep regex as fast path for obvious patterns, (2) fall back to LLM only when regex returns default RECENT and the question contains temporal language that regex can't parse. This hybrid approach limits LLM calls to ambiguous cases only.
files_to_modify: connectors/semantic_cache.py — add LLM fallback in detect_ttl_tier()
Prompt template for TTL classification (few-shot, constrained output)

implementation_checks: Regex still handles obvious patterns without LLM call
LLM fallback only triggers for ambiguous questions
Classification accuracy > 95% on a test set of 20 temporal questions
Latency impact < 300ms on LLM fallback path

verification.smoke_test: Test with ambiguous questions: 'How did Apple do before the pandemic?', 'What's happening with AAPL right now?', 'Compare last quarter to the one before'. Verify correct tier classification. Measure latency overhead.
verification.what_to_measure: Classification accuracy on ambiguous questions, latency of LLM fallback path
verification.prerequisite: Collect real usage data showing regex misclassification rates before prioritizing this phase

phases.title: Expose semantic cache distance for hits and near-misses (debug / tuning)
phases.estimated_effect: Easier threshold tuning and production debugging when investigating false hits or misses
phases.definition: Optional follow-up: extend semantic cache lookup so callers can read the best-match cosine distance (hits and near-misses) without duplicating DB queries. Use for structured logs, cache_meta SSE field, or admin metrics — not required for core cache behavior.
phases.explanation_for_agents: Today SemanticCache.lookup() returns only SemanticCacheEntry | None; distance is computed internally then discarded. If product wants visibility: (1) add lookup_with_distance() or optional return_distance on lookup, (2) on cache hit emit distance in cache_meta or logs only, (3) on miss optionally log best distance when below a debug-only second threshold to spot 'almost hits'. Keep lookup() unchanged for simple callers if adding a parallel method.
files_to_modify: connectors/semantic_cache.py — optional lookup_with_distance or extended return type
main.py — include distance in cache_meta or debug logs when cache-integration is present

implementation_checks: Hit path can attach best-match distance to logs or cache_meta without second round-trip to Postgres
Near-miss logging optional and gated (log level or env flag) to avoid noise
Default lookup API remains sufficient for production if team skips telemetry

verification.smoke_test: Run paraphrase pair from default_measurement_context; confirm logged or streamed distance matches manual SQL cosine distance check on semantic_cache row.
verification.what_to_measure: Log volume, correctness of reported distance vs pgvector query
